Comment: Beautiful vocals with a great folk influence for this cover/remix. So really nice stylistic transformation. Unfortunately, the original song doesn't have much in terms of melody rhythm of harmonics which feels very restrictive, On top of that, there doesn't seem to be much of a story, more of a jest,

A very straightforward song, however, it has excellent build by dramatic layering which support very well the narrative escalation. It's a clear message and it gets there in a very authentic way, even the melodic and harmonic monotony can't prevent a nice listening experience

Didn't know this song, so thank you for submitting. Very strong expressive delivery of a story full of great imagery and dramatic irony. The music is clearly the supporting cast, helping the dramatic escalation. I enjoyed listening to it.

Great production, a good hook with a unique vocal timbre. But the song as a whole or the story doesn't strike a chord with me. Probably doesn't help that It's been played zillions of times on the radio.

Didn't know this song from New Order, so thank you for submitting. The music however is very non-expressive for me, with very little tonal nuance and no development. So maybe that's just a way to focus the listener on the story, but also there I found it too literal for and ambiguous story.

Raw, gripping and honest. This indeed very much describes and makes you feel the sufferings of someone suffering from depression or psychosis. However, some form of this dialog is present in everyone. There is a lot of emotion in his delivery, and the simple guitar does a great job in supporting this great portrayal. Didn't know this song, so thank you for submitting.

A classic in storytelling and a great illustration of obsessive fans. For me, It lacks development musically to underpin the escalation in the story. but that's likely a stylistic choice. The delivery and flow however is unmatched. A great song.
I didn't find much of a story in this one, and the music didn't do much for me either, unfortunately. One-dimensional, conventional, mediocre performance...
Predictable story, but nicely done and enjoyable. Some nice variation in flow of lyrics. Musically, not much to get here except an overall relaxed feeling.

a fun story AND a good song? Apparently it can be done. This is the perfect combination of Jack Black's comedic and musical genius. great build and escalation as the story unfolds, great variety and contrast in melody and rhythm. For my taste they could have worked in a bit more harmonic variation, but that's very clearly a stylistic choice.
Great production that develops the songs soundscape. But besides that, extremely conventional and cliché in all aspects. Not for me. Also, didn't find a real story

Good song, original, but it feels more as a documentary than a song. It brings across the story very factual, but for me lacks the musical connection to also bring the emotion across. Didn't know this song, so thank you for submitting.
Meh... powerful subject, but it didn't make me feel anything. It has some development, but the harmonics, rhythm, melody and vocal delivery are very static.

A true pop culture icon, referenced countless times. Also, apparently Avril still looks the same. For me, it's ok to listen to, but it's conventional in every way. It has a chord progression of 8 chords instead of 4, that's something :) The story clear and understandable, but ultimately doesn't move me

I loved this song so much in high school! I can't tell you how much better it sounds with two 12" subwoofers in the trunk of a hatchback. More bass than you would think when listening on regular speakers. And the story felt so meaningful at the time as well.
I would have voted for the Weeknd version...
This does bring back memories from my MTV time.
What a sad story..
The medieval theme does sound like a great story is being told. Funny cover from the Weeknd. But I found the story lacking depth.
I have also wondered what, as a parent, I should do. Sometimes the 'correct' path isn't the safest for your family. What is the correct path? Would I dare to take it?
I'm already living as common people....
Funny story! Did not know this one.
I forgot about this one! I really laughed the first time I heard this song.
Strong story!
Nice build up of the music, but no points for this one.
I love how absolutely meta that last verse is.
Story is pretty good, but the singing is (gently put) incredibly meh.
I thought it started comedic, but then it wasn't funny anymore after that :( Nice take on the song and nice singing but according to the prompt there was not enough story here to earn a point.
Courageous to pick a Dutch song, but I really appreciated this one. I hope the others looked up a translation, and if not before voting consider doing it now anyway. Worth it.
Great story!
I'm upset at the open ending -_- Otherwise great song!
After all the memes and gags I finally took the time to properly listen to this one. Lyrics drip of remembrence and nostalgia which is kinda nice. Hard to get past how overplayed this song is tho :(

I found out about this one quite recently and submitted it two leagues ago. Then I found out it was submitted to one of the few leagues I missed so it lacked the impact of surprise. What a trip though, eh? Point for a like minded spirit!

Emma thought I would submit this one. I would've but I already had other plans. Amazing song that still maintains Eminems style, while also humanizing him in a good way. I think it was the first song where I thought he was a decent human being behind the ganster facade.
Story is a bit too vague... did he push her off a cliff into the ocean and regretted it later? Or am I just thinking way too dark here ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
Perfect story with a twist! Relationship is doomed though since both were willing to cheat their way out.

While I try to stay away from the popular charts, I couldn't pass up this Tenacious D gem. Upon further inspection though... it only hit the charts in the UK, NZ, AUS and of course NL :D USA didn't like it that much, eh?
Not quite a story. Fine lyrics about the nature of photographs, though. There might be a story buried underneath but it's up to everyones personal interpretation.

Not really a story, more like a suicide note. Good song, but controversial. I just went to an inspirational evening on how to raise teenagers with Emma and themes like this scare me nowadays. My kids are fine, but as a parent these "messages" and signs hit harder, I think.
A beautiful little story about a lost love, by Robert Smith who's been happily dating and married to his high school girlfriend for 50 years
A favorite of mine.  I always thought of it as a ghost story.  Is the war as righteous as the unreliable narrator thinks?  I feel like it's a commentary on the human cost of 'patriotism'.

I liked how she changed the lyrics to Ser Weeknd's song to have a little more story to it.  Might not be as much of a story as some others on the playlist, but with two songs per round I want to try to pick more fun stuff

I really appreciate the effort here in blending genres while still being a Beyoncé song. Yes, it's still a pop song with straightforward progression and harmonics, it mainly borrows the instruments and sound, and not so much the structure. But still, it's creative, and I always enjoy seeing people try something new. What I really missed is her vocals. She has an amazing voice, but hardly used it in this song...

I'm conflicted on this one. It's great vocal and orchestral performance, and it's an emotional song. Sure, after the first crescendo, the rest of the song is a bit too predictable for my taste, but on paper, I should have really enjoyed listening to it, but instead I didn't feel too much either way. Maybe it was because I felt his voice lost its emotional power when the song started to develop in intensity.

Two Ozzy ballets? Come on... we can do better than that.  A nice ballet nonetheless, and for me, one of his better songs. It's very warm with great timbre with an iconic bridge. But it also lacks for me the vocal performance and virtuosity to really deliver an emotional message. The melody is enjoyable, but the fairly straightforward harmonics and standard rhythm keep it from being an excellent song for me.

meh meh meh... really wanted to like this song as it is very different from their normal work, but the vocals are just so inconsistent and it's such a linear song. I did enjoy the arrangement and instrumental performance.

Sting and Herbie, what a magical mashup. Great reinterpretation of a classic. Intimate, and harmonically sophisticated. It really puts the skills of the artists on display with basically the majority of the song being just String and Herbie with a subtle Double bass as backdrop. Great stuff
I like this exploration, but it's so static and repetitive with virtually no development. And the production is so flat and tight, like I'm listening to a metronome.

Fun to listen to for a bit, and they're definitely able to recreate this rockabilly feel. But they did a pretty much 1:1 rendition of the original song with very little innovation in arrangement, performance, harmonic, whatever. So yeah, it's ok

Yes, she did capture the psychedelic groovy 60's vibe, but It doesn't feel like a big departure from her other work for me. It has a nice and powerful hook, but that's pretty much the whole song. Just too repetitive to enjoy it past the first 30 seconds (those first 30 seconds I really enjoyed)
Yup, it's a pop song. It was difficult to find anything in here that I liked.

Nice fusion between K-pop and hardstyle/EDM. Very driving hardstyle beat, making this ready to be played in the club. It's creative and confident. And for a hardstyle/EDM song, I quite liked it, even tho it was very hook-driven.

Two Ozzy ballets? Come on... we can do better than that. But unlike in the other one, there isn't much here for me. Literal lyrics, repetitive harmonics and structure with a weak melody. Also, for me, it doesn't feel cohesive.
Perfect analysis and description of a 00's pop song. Very mediocre, repetitive song, but it was funny, so points for that

Very creative, mixing a lot of genres that weren't mixed before. Interesting listening. It has a nice funk feel throughout, with some solos, but it is fairly harmonically underdeveloped and repetitive. It could have been so much more...
Nice vocal delivery, but the song relies a bit too much on this vocal delivery, with everything else being fairly underdeveloped. It does have a nice Americana feel to it.

I didn't see the explicit label really add anything to the song. There were a lot of fucks, a couple of bitches, but very little emotional range. The music didn't do anything for me. Nothing wrong with it, but everything was just ok.

Iconic chorus with great drums and guitar. Some nice build throughout the song, building on a clear center point, being the chorus. A clear display of frustration, where the explicit nature of the song adds to expressing that.

Wow.... what a mashup, different styles, different genres, different languages, and somehow they made it into a coherent song. So creative! The only nitpick for me would be that the harmonics are fairly straightforward. Yes, this holds it together, but it could have done that with less straightforward harmonics as well. But lush and great harmonics. The explicit part adds something, although not too much
Fun song with a great message. The song is not very remarkable, but the rap has a nice flow which made it an enjoyable listen. The explicit part used to good effect.

Culturally, a big impart on artistic freedom. The message is clear, and it has a good hook, but besides that there isn't much there for me. The lyrics aren't even that bad, but the delivery is horrible.

yeah, it's simple and straightforward, yes it has limited melodic and harmonic richness, but at least it puts that to good use by focusing on emotional delivery, which I can appreciate. And the explicit label allows the artist to express themselves more broadly.
